hard.next_step: 8th of July - Confidence : 90% Florence CPO hired one of our senior consultant who will be PM for her he will join end of August Project in 2 phases (Contract first / Supplier on boarding 2nd) Project Start end of augues / Go live this calendar year for Phase1 She wants to sign before end of July (She is in vacation in 2 weeks) NS Sent the SoW following this call => 9th of July Commercial call with Praaven (replace Émeline as she is off) => 9th or 10th of July Demo focus Merlin for I Contract => 10gh of July at 10AM TBC (Varun Kukreja) Finalizaion of contracts => 24th of July 29th of June - Confidence : 90% Good call with Émeline she did not came back on Merlin for I Contract to be put as option instead of core module which would have a significant impact (50K if we consider discount) on TCV (let's see if Florence will insist or not) we are almost there for OF1, Advanced support options to be added (good news potential additional revenue ! ) and 2 minor typos / format to be changed. If we can have this by tomorrow that would be great. For closing plan we are aligned target is still mid of July. 24th of June - Confidence : 80% BAFO sent today Negotiation meeting scheduled on 25th of June Second round of legal comments should be received from Publicis by 25th of June Questionnaire to finalize SoW / OF2 should be received by 25th of June => Close plan - Final version for final review of contractual package to be sent by Zycus on 29th / 30th of June => Comments / last negotiation calls from 1st of July till 10th of July => Signature target date : 15th of July => Project start last week of July 12th of June - Confidence : 80% Publicis : Positive => Émeline (purchaser) super pushy - already 4 version of OF1 - we included IAnalyze as option could significantly increase the deal size / Supplier creation process launched - Legal : OK were supposed to send first remark this week will be more probably early next week (Émeline is off, she will be back Monday) so not perfectly on time but ok for legal Questionnable (not negative at this stage) => Willingness of Stephen Cook (Head of procurement performance (so in charge of processes and tools) and Paras (external consultant important in tech validation) to move forward as I sent 3 reminders asking them to schedule a WS to finalize contract / John one => 0 Feedback. Action : I already sent them a 4th reminder with questionnaire from Hrishikesh for them to prepare this WS if no answer by Monday afternoon we need to escalate (direct call to Florence, ask for a call with Aatish...others.. to be discussed) 4st of june - Confidence : 80% We answered with our completed OF1 to 3 points. NS and agreed close plan 5th of june : send a new OF1 to answer to Emeline requests to reduce the budget for FY26 - We should have an acceptable answer Starting 8th of June : Start legal discusssion on MSA (no clear close plan / no contact with Legal at this stage) 8th of June : Publicis following our presentation on 2nd of June should come back to us with additional questions to ensure that the high level vision of our champion Florence is really aligned with their contractual processes (that's the main risk) Between 8th nd 12th of June : Schedule a 90 minutes WS to be able to create a SoW (OF2) to be sent around 15th of June Joined target shared by Publicis and Zycus : Start the project in July for a go live (quick win) before end of FY26 => Need to finalize agreement end of June / First week of July (at this stage I let our close date in July) => Secure this close plan with a Top Management Call Aatish and Florence / Need first to have their questions and have a good knowledge about potential blockers so this call will not be before 15th of June. 1st of June - Confidence : 80% Starting Contractual discussion at the end of our call today. 4 points to be addressed. Still need to Come back with OF1 completed asap (with clear answer on 4 points) Schedule a SoW WS in the coming days / Prior to this Publicis to come back with additional questions that could oblige us to make a second presentations based on their uses cases - Close plan not entirely clear depending of the list of questions we will receive in the coming days / Florence as champion confirmed her willingness to close asap and have a quick win (solution in production) before end of 26. => See how we will have to involve Aatish for a call with Florence to ensure our full support (at this stage I think we need first to have their feedback so additional questions and us to send OF1 before to have an efficient call with clear take away). 26th May - Vendor Selected 21st of May - Confidence : 80% Next steps - Shared with Publicis Contract In order to send you two order forms - one for the subscription, OF1, and one for the implementation, OF2, we would need to organize a short 90-minute workshop with your technical team. The objective would be to validate all assumptions and ensure that OF2 is fully aligned with Publicis’ expectations. This workshop can be organized as soon as possible. Once it has taken place, we will need four working days to finalize OF1 and OF2 and send them to you for review. A possible retro-planning could therefore be: Workshop next week OF1 and OF2 sent by 5 June for review Target to finalize the agreement before the end of June Project start in July Go-live before the end of 2026 11th of May - Confidence : 40% Assumptions for pricing sent / Need confirmation from CPO (Florence) + input on number of existing contracts PS Estimation needs to be done by 15th of may Proposal to be sent on 15th of May / WS to refine implementation time line and assumptions to be scheduled end of May. First version of OF1 / OF2 mid of June / Negotiation process : Target date signature mid of Juy.
hard.ais_why:
• SF Description identifies Florence as 'a strong CPO champion' from PwC where Zycus deployed ‚Äî a named CPO who has prior Zycus experience and is now driving the Publicis evaluation. Product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake within the CLM/iRequest stack (iContract, iRequest, iSupplier, Merlin, Merlin Intake). The differentiation narrative names AI-driven sourcing, autonomous negotiation, and automated compliance.
• Qualified stage with proposal sent 1 May 2026 and target signature mid-July. 4 prior Avoma meetings (May/Sep 2025) with Cedric Elmaleh predate Florence's CPO arrival and are not directly relevant. The current cycle is partner-led (Bertrand intro) with no recorded Florence call yet on this specific opp.
• Florence's compelling event per Description is a year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact, not specifically AI; the deal is structured as a quick-win CLM/Intake replication of the PwC blueprint. Pushed UP from prior 5 to 6 based on the rich Description + Merlin in scope + named CPO champion with prior Zycus experience. Score 6 reflects Florence's potential AI championship without explicit AI-led mandate captured yet.

north_star_verdict.headline: Publicis is racing to signature: after the constructive 8 Jul review with Florence Tinsley-Roy, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun, the full contract pack (MSA, OF1, SoW) is in the buyer's hands, finalization is targeted 24 Jul, and the CPO wants to sign before her late-July vacation; the one thing between us and the win is landing SoW and Merlin-scope sign-off at the buyer-proposed 13 Jul review.
north_star_verdict.math: 21 days to close (CloseDate 2026-07-30, today 2026-07-09). Vendor Selected since 26 May 2026 (44 days in stage) but the paper trail (BAFO 24 Jun, MSA legal clauses resolved 29 Jun, final OF1 30 Jun, SoW delivered 8 Jul) shows the deal operating at contract finalization. Close moved 13 to 15 Jul (29 Jun) then 15 to 30 Jul (8 Jul), matching Florence's stated sign-before-end-of-July target rather than an uncontrolled slip. Remaining pace: SoW and Merlin review 13 Jul, contract finalization 24 Jul, signature by 30 Jul; achievable but with zero slack once Florence's vacation starts around 22 Jul.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Commit (hold); advance StageName to Negotiation / Contract In Progress to match the paper evidence
evidence: 8 Jul 2026: constructive review call with Florence Tinsley-Roy, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun; Zycus sent the SoW the same day, so Publicis now holds MSA, OF1 and SoW (SF activity emails 8 Jul 2026).
8 Jul 2026 next step: Florence hired a senior Zycus consultant as her incoming project manager (joins end of August) and wants to sign before end of July, ahead of her vacation two weeks out.

Buyer-proposed next milestone: Praveen Seemundun asked for Monday 13 Jul 11:30 BST for the SoW and Merlin for iContract review (SF activity email 8 Jul 2026); contract finalization targeted 24 Jul (Next_Step_History__c 8 Jul 2026).

Paper and compliance gates cleared in parallel: NDA executed 29 Jun, MSA legal clauses resolved 29 Jun, OneTrust self-assessment completed 2 Jul, Ariba supplier creation underway since 22 Jun (SF activity emails).

critical_signals.text: The 8 Jul review with Florence Tinsley-Roy, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun was constructive: Florence confirmed she wants to sign before her late-July vacation and has already hired a senior consultant as her project manager starting end of August.

Publicis now holds the full contract pack (MSA, OF1 and the SoW sent 8 Jul) with finalization targeted 24 Jul, but whether Merlin for iContract stays a core module or drops to an option, worth about 50K of the 250K, is still open since 29 Jun.

Praveen Seemundun, Category Manager IT Services at Publicis Re:Sources, has taken over the commercial thread while Emeline Le Foyer is on vacation, and he is actively engaged: he proposed the 13 Jul review himself and runs the compliance and supplier-onboarding gates.
Sole-source: no rival has been named anywhere from 11 May through 8 Jul; Florence selected Zycus off her prior PwC deployment experience and went straight to contracting without a bake-off.

metrics.narrative: The pain is explicit but never quantified: MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) records 'actual contractual process is a mess / email validation / no dashboard / no possibility to know easily what are the next steps', and no cost, headcount or cycle-time figure has been voiced by the buyer in any email or next-step entry through 8 Jul 2026. The value frame that is carrying the deal is Florence's year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact (SF Description) with a phase-1 go-live this calendar year (Next_Step_History__c 8 Jul 2026). Commercial value levers surfaced along the way: IAnalyze added as an option (12 Jun) and advanced support options added for additional revenue (29 Jun), against a possible 50K reduction if Merlin for iContract is optioned. At this contracting stage the number is negotiated rather than ROI-modelled; no buyer-built business case exists on record.

economic_buyer.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy, Chief Procurement Officer, is the economic buyer and is directly engaged: she is the primary Decision Maker contact role, MEDDPICC 2.0 (4 Jun 2026) confirms the decision is hers ('For Economic buyer decision is already done'), and she personally emailed on 6 Jul to reschedule the review ('Can we please move to 11am Tuesday or Wednesday?') before attending the 8 Jul call. Her authority shows in actions: she set the signature deadline against her own vacation ('She wants to sign before end of July', Next_Step_History__c 8 Jul 2026) and hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project manager before any signature. She has been the north star of every close-plan entry from 21 May through 8 Jul.

decision_criteria.narrative: The criteria are fit with Publicis's existing contractual processes, a quick-win two-phase implementation (contracts first, supplier onboarding second) live this calendar year, and staying inside a constrained FY26 budget (Emeline asked for a new OF1 'to reduce the budget for FY26', Next_Step_History__c 4 Jun 2026). Process fit was the open validation: Stephen Cook (Head of Procurement Performance) and Paras Sood (external consultant for technology validation) had to confirm alignment with existing processes (MEDDPICC 2.0, 4 Jun 2026), stalled from 12 Jun through four reminders, then completed: Emeline returned the functional questionnaire on 1 Jul ('the document completed on the functional part... might be adjusted pending on new operating model') and both validators joined the constructive 8 Jul review. The remaining criterion in play is whether Merlin for iContract earns core-module status; the 13 Jul demo is the proof point.
sources: SF activity emails 1 Jul and 8 Jul 2026

decision_process.narrative: No RFP; Publicis went straight from Florence's selection to contracting (MEDDPICC 2.0, 4 Jun 2026). The activity sequence maps the process: pricing assumptions (11 May), close-plan workshop framing (21 May), Vendor Selected (26 May), contractual discussion opens (1 Jun), four OF1 iterations driven by Emeline (4 to 12 Jun), BAFO and a negotiation meeting (24 to 25 Jun), MSA convergence (26 to 29 Jun), SoW delivery after the group review (8 Jul), finalization targeted 24 Jul. Approval is EB-led with two parallel gates: process validation by Stephen Cook and Paras Sood (cleared at the 8 Jul review) and procurement/compliance run by the relevant stakeholder through OneTrust and Ariba supplier creation (22 Jun to 2 Jul). Signature is planned before Florence's vacation, around 22 to 30 Jul.
sources: SF activity emails 22 Jun to 8 Jul 2026

paper_process.narrative: Standard new-business relay, well advanced: NDA executed via the relevant stakeholder 29 Jun; MSA redlines converged (Publicis legal returned its revised document 26 Jun asking Zycus to 'get closer to executable version', and Zycus returned the revised MSA 29 Jun stating 'All legal clauses have now been resolved', pending the buyer's final review); final OF1 sent 30 Jun with typo fixes and advanced support options; the SoW (OF2) was delivered 8 Jul, so the buyer holds the complete pack ('You now have all the contractual documents: MSA, OF1, and SoW'). Compliance runs in parallel: the OneTrust self-assessment was completed 2 Jul and Ariba supplier creation has been underway since 22 Jun. SoW agreement is the classic signature predictor and is the last gate: review is set for 13 Jul with finalization targeted 24 Jul. France is a direct-invoicing region, so no PO gate is expected.
sources: SF activity emails 26 Jun to 8 Jul 2026

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and consistently repeated: Publicis's contractual process is manual and email-based, with no dashboard and no visibility of next steps (MEDDPICC 2.0, 4 Jun 2026: 'actual contractual process is a mess / email validation / no dashboard / no possibility to know easily what are the next steps'). The pain owner is Florence Tinsley-Roy's procurement organization; Emeline Le Foyer was the operational driver pushing four OF1 rounds to fix commercial terms around it. The forcing function is Florence's year-end mandate to demonstrate procurement impact (SF Description), which is why the deal is structured as a quick-win phase-1 go-live this calendar year.

champion.narrative: Florence Tinsley-Roy is a strong, active champion and economic buyer in one: she selected Zycus off her prior PwC deployment experience (SF Description), personally rescheduled the review rather than delegate it (6 Jul email), attended the 8 Jul call, drives the timeline against her own vacation, and hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project manager before signature (Next_Step_History__c 8 Jul 2026), organizational skin in the game no passive sponsor shows. Emeline Le Foyer is the secondary operational advocate, described as 'super pushy' while driving four OF1 revisions (12 Jun), though she is on vacation for the final mile with the relevant stakeholder stepping in. No cooling signal exists; advocacy is escalating.

competition.narrative: This is a genuinely sole-sourced deal: MEDDPICC 2.0 records 'No competition' (4 Jun 2026), no rival appears in any Salesforce field, email, or next-step entry from 11 May through 8 Jul 2026, and Publicis moved straight from Florence's selection to BAFO and contracting without a competitive bake-off. The buyer chose Zycus on the strength of the PwC blueprint Florence lived with as a prior customer. The competitive risk is therefore not a rival but scope-shaving: Emeline's proposal to demote Merlin for iContract from core to option (29 Jun) is the live threat to deal value, and the 13 Jul Merlin review is where Zycus defends it.

deal_movement.summary: Continuous forward motion since selection on 26 May 2026: amount raised to 250K and forecast to Commit (1 Jun), BAFO sent (24 Jun) with a negotiation meeting (25 Jun), legal converged (26 to 29 Jun), compliance cleared (2 Jul), and the SoW delivered hours after the constructive 8 Jul group review. The countermove: the close date was pushed twice in ten days, landing on 30 Jul, though it matches Florence's own sign-before-end-of-July target.

items.change: Stage set to Vendor Selected; forecast to Best Case
Amount raised 230K to 250K; forecast upgraded Best Case to Commit
BAFO sent; negotiation meeting held 25 Jun
Publicis legal returned its revised contract asking for a near-executable version
Revised MSA sent with all legal clauses resolved; NDA fully executed; close date 13 to 15 Jul
OneTrust compliance self-assessment completed by Zycus
Constructive group review (Florence, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood, Praveen); SoW sent, completing the contract pack; close date 15 to 30 Jul

day_summary.overall: The 8 Jul review call brought Florence Tinsley-Roy, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun together and was, in the same-day thank-you to the group, constructive: contracts are to be finalized by 24 Jul, the project runs in two phases starting end of August, and Florence intends to sign before her vacation. Zycus followed through within hours and sent the SoW, so Publicis now holds the full contract pack (MSA, OF1 and SoW). Praveen then proposed Monday 13 Jul for the SoW and Merlin review, and the close date was reset to 30 Jul in line with Florence's end-of-July signature target. The day's note also recorded that Florence has hired a senior Zycus consultant as her incoming project manager, joining end of August.

competitive_position.summary: Sole-sourced, and the evidence says genuinely so rather than a visibility gap: MEDDPICC 2.0 records 'No competition' (4 Jun 2026), no rival is named in any field, email or next-step entry from 11 May through 8 Jul 2026, and the buyer ran no RFP, moving straight from Florence Tinsley-Roy's selection (built on her prior PwC deployment of Zycus) to BAFO and contracting. There is no do-nothing risk either: Publicis is actively contracting, running its own compliance and supplier-onboarding machinery for Zycus. The single current threat to deal value is internal scope-shaving, Emeline's 29 Jun proposal to demote Merlin for iContract from core module to option (about 50K of the 250K TCV), which Zycus defends at the 13 Jul Merlin review.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Publicis is buying a quick-win replication of the PwC blueprint: phase 1 (contracts) live this calendar year, inside a constrained FY26 budget, fitted to their existing contractual processes. Alignment is strong on timeline, budget and process fit after the 8 Jul review; the exposed edge is proving Merlin for iContract deserves core-module status.

best_practice_check.summary: Winning posture on a sole-source Commit deal; the levers that protect the win are continuity through two simultaneous vacations and defending scope on value rather than price.
flags: Multi-thread the final mile: give Praveen Seemundun, Stephen Cook and Paras Sood each a named close-plan task so the deal survives Florence's vacation from around 22 Jul.
Defend the 50K Merlin for iContract scope on value: anchor it to Florence's year-end procurement-impact mandate and the PwC blueprint at the 13 Jul demo, never on discount.
Guard the commercial envelope won on 29 Jun: any further movement should trade term or phasing, not the advanced-support upsell or core modules.

The 13 Jul Merlin review with Varun Kukreja is the moment to land the agentic story with Florence's team and lock the AI scope into the contract.

The 1 Jul request for Merlin information and the scheduled 13 Jul demo show genuine but price-sensitive AI interest.

deal_momentum.text: Florence (CPO/EB) personally hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project PM ahead of signing and set her own signature deadline before her vacation (Next Step, 8 Jul) — real forward motion, not rep push.
Fast paper trail in the last 3 weeks: BAFO sent 24 Jun → negotiation call 25 Jun → two rounds of legal comments → SoW sent 8 Jul → contract finalization dated 24 Jul.
Rep-logged confidence rose 80%→90% across the 12 Jun–8 Jul entries, with concrete forward dates (demo 10 Jul, commercial call w/ Praveen 9-10 Jul).

None of this is corroborated by the CRM meeting record — 0 meetings logged in 30/60/90 days, last logged meeting is Sep-2025 (282 days). ► Log the Jun-Jul calls (Florence, Émeline, Praveen) as Completed Tasks so momentum isn't invisible in the system of record.

Close date has already slipped once (mid-Jul → 30 Jul) and Florence's vacation starts ~22 Jul, tightening the runway to the 24 Jul finalization. ► Lock the 24 Jul call now with written confirmation before she leaves.

win_position.text: Genuinely sole-sourced: MEDDPICC records 'no competition,' no rival named May-Jul, Publicis went straight from Florence's PwC-based selection to BAFO/contracting.
Champion and economic buyer are the same person — Florence drove the process herself, hired the PM, and set her own signing deadline (full direct exec access, not delegated).
Commit forecast at Vendor Selected is backed by hard paper-process evidence — BAFO sent, two rounds of legal comments, OF1 near-final, SoW sent, contract finalization scheduled 24 Jul.

Merlin for iContract (core vs. option) is still unresolved and could cut ~$50K off the $250K TCV; Émeline never came back on it. ► Get Florence's explicit ruling on Merlin scope before the 24 Jul close.

Single-threaded at exec level — Florence is the only confirmed executive contact; Émeline is on leave and Stephen Cook/Paras needed four reminders before engaging. ► Build a parallel relationship with Stephen Cook to de-risk the Florence-only thread.

customer_commitment.text: Florence hired a senior Zycus consultant as her own PM before contracts are signed — real budget/headcount commitment on the buyer side.
Buyer-driven paper process: supplier-onboarding launched, two rounds of legal comments exchanged, OF1 nearly finalized.
Florence set her own signature deadline (before end-July, ahead of her own vacation) — self-imposed urgency.

Stephen Cook and Paras Sood (process/tech validation) went dark for three weeks despite four reminders before joining the 8 Jul review. ► Confirm they're actively signing off, not just present on one call.

scope_change.to: 250K with IAnalyze as an added option and advanced support options included
scope_change.detail: Amount raised 230K to 250K on 1 Jun 2026; IAnalyze added as an option that could increase deal size (12 Jun) and advanced support options added as additional revenue (29 Jun). Watch item: Emeline proposed moving Merlin for iContract from core to option, which would cut about 50K; unresolved since 29 Jun.

customer_preference.evidence: Sole-source selection with no rival named anywhere; Florence 'wants to sign before end of July' and hired the project PM before signature (Next Step 8 Jul); Praveen proposed the next review himself ('Does Monday 11.30 am BST work for you?', 8 Jul email).

business_case.evidence: Pain is qualitative only ('actual contractual process is a mess / email validation / no dashboard', MEDDPICC 2.0, 4 Jun 2026); no buyer-quantified ROI. The value frame is Florence's year-end procurement-impact mandate and a phase-1 go-live this calendar year.

cro_panel.header: Publicis Groupe — Vendor Selected · $250K · closes 30 Jun
cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. Strong on paper for the stage, but it's stalling — the 'what could lose it' block is the real story.

blocks.title: Zycus win position
blocks.read: Late-stage by the book, but the signals underneath are thin.

bullets.text: Close date pushed twice in ten days (13 to 15 Jul on 29 Jun, then 15 to 30 Jul on 8 Jul); Florence's vacation from around 22 Jul leaves no slack behind the 24…
Merlin for iContract core versus option is unresolved; optioning it would cut about 50K from the 250K TCV.

blocks.title: Deal momentum
blocks.read: Accelerating — one of the hotter deals in the book.

blocks.title: What could lose it
blocks.read: The threat is the deal stalling into 'do nothing', not a competitor beating us.

bullets.text: Florence is the only contact-role stakeholder and sole executive thread, while Emeline, the commercial driver, is on vacation and Praveen Seemundun carries the thread…
bullets.full: Florence is the only contact-role stakeholder and sole executive thread, while Emeline, the commercial driver, is on vacation and Praveen Seemundun carries the thread without the deal's history.

blocks.footer: Competitive picture: Sole-sourced, and the evidence says genuinely so rather than a visibility gap: MEDDPICC 2.0 records 'No competition' (4 Jun 2026), no rival is named in any field, email or next-step entry from 11 May through 8 Jul 2026, and the buyer ran…

blocks.title: What moves it forward
items: Confirm signatories, e-sign route and signing dates with Praveen at the 13 Jul review. (by 13 Jun)
Secure Florence's written confirmation that Merlin for iContract stays core scope after the demo. (by 15 Jun)
Book an executive connect with the deal owner's manager and Florence before her vacation. (by 20 Jun)

calls_omitted: Zycus Procurement AI World Tour: Paris Exclusive Dinner Event (2025-09-30): no transcript or notes available; predates the current cycle (background only).
evidence_coverage.salesforce_window: activities last 120 days; field history 365 days; full Next_Step_History__c trail (11 May to 8 Jul 2026)
gaps: No recorded/transcribed buyer call exists for the current cycle (May to Jul 2026); the 1 Jun demo, 19 Jun catch-up, 6 Jul and 8 Jul reviews are logged as Clari events without Avoma recordings, so meeting content is reconstructed from the dense two-way email trail and the Next Step journal (knowledge gap).
No buyer-quantified ROI or metrics voiced in any source (knowledge gap).
validation gate: a named person in a to-do/action item is in no Salesforce contact role, Avoma attendee list, or active-user roster and is anchored to no source (offending: 'Varun Kukreja')
